test.note: Known pre-existing issue: test target is a skeleton with no test files on disk. AGENTS.md documents this as out of scope. Not related to this milestone.

blockingIssues.description: Streaming logprobs format mismatch in SSEEncoder.textCompletionStream: uses chat completions format (logprobs.content array of {token, logprob, top_logprobs} objects) instead of legacy completions format (tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset as separate arrays). The non-streaming path correctly uses the legacy format. This inconsistency breaks lm-eval's parse_logprobs() for streaming responses.

nonBlockingIssues.description: text_offset uses lp.token.utf8.count (byte offsets) instead of character offsets. OpenAI spec describes text_offset as character offsets. For multi-byte Unicode the values would diverge, but ASCII-heavy lm-eval datasets make this low severity.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit boundary: Both streaming and non-streaming /v1/completions logprobs responses MUST use the legacy format (tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset). Do NOT use the chat completions logprobs.content format in the completions endpoint.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature completions-logprobs-forwarding used chat format for streaming, creating inconsistency with non-streaming path. architecture.md invariant #6 'Legacy format exactness' only describes the shape but doesn't explicitly call out that streaming must also follow this format.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Expand invariant #6 'Legacy format exactness' to explicitly state that BOTH streaming and non-streaming paths must use the legacy format, and that the chat completions logprobs.content format must NOT be used in the completions endpoint.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The current invariant only describes the shape without specifying it applies to streaming too, which allowed the inconsistent implementation.
